spec_paraphrase: SVD-LLM V2 listed as Stage 3 motivation paper for heterogeneous rank allocation.
paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM has two limitations. First, SVD-LLM applies a homogeneous compression ratio to all the weight matrices. ... Second, SVD-LLM utilizes Cholesky decomposition for weight truncation.
notes: Spec cites V2 only as motivation; the homogeneous-ratio limitation is the canonical motivator the spec reuses for non-uniform rank allocation.

paper_verbatim: First, SVD-LLM V2 proposes to use theoretical truncation loss ... Second, SVD-LLM V2 proposes loss-optimized weight truncation
notes: Spec uses D-Rank (2509.25622) for rank allocation and AA-SVD (2604.02119) for truncation, not V2's two techniques. V2 is motivation only.

paper_verbatim: L = ||W X − W'X||_F
notes: Generic Frobenius truncation-loss objective; spec relies on AA-SVD's equivalent formulation, not V2's.

spec_paraphrase: Spec groups by (layer, matrix_type) and applies per-projection rank bias gate=1.33/up=0.67/down=1.0 — conceptually a per-type rank allocation in the spirit of V2.
paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 groups the weight matrices across all the layers in the original LLM by type, such as query (Q) and key (K) in attention blocks, and Gate (G) and Up (U) in MLP blocks.
notes: Spec's grouping is per (layer, matrix_type) pair — finer than V2's per-type-across-all-layers. V2 is cited as motivation; D7a's actual numerical recipe is sourced from jangq's GGUF, not from V2.

paper_verbatim: procedure RATIO_ALLOCATION(M, S, R): G ← Group(M); ... r ← Len(LG) × R × Lmin/Sum(LG)
notes: Spec uses D-Rank Eq. 7 (spectral entropy) for rank allocation, not V2's Algorithm 1.

paper_verbatim: r ← Len(LG) × R × Lmin/Sum(LG)
notes: Per-matrix ratio formula not used; D-Rank Eq. 7 used instead.

paper_verbatim: Lmin = ||C − C'||_F, where C is the original matrix of W X and C' is its compressed version by SVD ... It then inverses and normalizes Lmin by 1/Log(Lmin).
notes: V2's Lmin proxy not used; spec computes effective rank via spectral entropy of B-eigenvalues instead.

paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 bypasses the need to measure end-to-end perplexity to determine compression ratios
notes: Spec does use WikiText-2 PPL search for Swift-SVD+ α (D9 resolved 2026-04-30); but this is unrelated to V2's claim.

spec_paraphrase: Per-projection rank bias is justified in part by the empirical evidence that truncation loss varies across layers/types — exactly the V2 motivation.
paper_verbatim: the truncation loss varies at different layers. For example, the query matrix in the 27th layer has a much higher truncation loss than that of the first layer
notes: This is the canonical motivation reference. Spec's D7a TODO notes the gate:up:down ratios are 'theoretically- (not empirically-) grounded' and need re-tune from clean per-projection recon_rel_err.

paper_verbatim: different types of weight matrices, such as query (Q) and key (K) in attention blocks, and Gate (G) and Up (U) in MLP blocks
notes: V/O in attention and Down in MLP are implicit in V2's scheme — paper exemplifies but does not enumerate all 7 LLaMA projections. Captured per audit instruction (V/O/Down implicit-grouping caveat).

paper_verbatim: procedure WEIGHT_TRUNCATION(W, X, R): S ← XX^T; Us, Ss, Vs ← SVD(S); D ← W × Us × √Ss; Uws, Sws, Vws ← SVD(D); Ts ← Truncate(Sws, R); W' ← Uws × Ts × S^{-1}_s × U^{-1}_s
notes: Spec uses AA-SVD (2604.02119) Theorem 3.2 / Corollary 3.3 for truncation, not V2's two-SVD procedure.

paper_verbatim: the compressed weight matrix W' = Uws × Trunc.(Sws) × Vws × √Ss^{-1} × U_s^{-1} ensures the theoretical minimum truncation loss.
notes: V2 optimality theorem not used by spec.
Proof internals not used.
Proof internals not used.

paper_verbatim: because XX^T is not positive definite, SVD-LLM fails to compress matrix A. ... and this difference even increases with increasing compression ratio.
notes: Cholesky-failure motivation; spec uses AA-SVD eigendecomposition path which has no Cholesky.

paper_verbatim: MATRIX A RATIO 20%: Theoretical 0.5982; SVD-LLM Fail; SVD-LLM V2 0.5982
notes: Empirical numerical-stability table; not referenced by spec.

paper_verbatim: MATRIX A RATIO 60%: Theoretical 2.3251; SVD-LLM Fail; SVD-LLM V2 2.3251
notes: Same as tab1_matA_20.

paper_verbatim: MATRIX B RATIO 20%: Theoretical 0.7351; SVD-LLM 0.8961; SVD-LLM V2 0.7351 (↓18%)
notes: Same.

paper_verbatim: MATRIX B RATIO 60%: Theoretical 3.5245; SVD-LLM 5.9834; SVD-LLM V2 3.5245 (↓41%)
notes: Same.

paper_verbatim: We randomly select 256 WikiText-2 samples as the calibration data.
notes: Spec uses Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data multi-domain calibration (D11 deviation, sanctioned) — but that's a deviation against 2603.02217, not V2.

paper_verbatim: we followed the implementation of SVD-LLM (Wang et al., 2024b) to add the small noise into the decomposed matrices.
notes: V1-baseline noise tweak; not relevant.

paper_verbatim: The compression ration in our experiments refers to the parameter reduction of LLM achieved through compression.
notes: Definitional convention; not used.

paper_verbatim: All of the experiments are conducted on A100 GPUs.
notes: Paper hardware (A100) differs from spec (H200); irrelevant since spec doesn't reproduce V2 numbers.

paper_verbatim: we evaluate the performance of SVD-LLM V2 on five models at various scales
notes: Eval models not used (spec targets Qwen3-30B MoE).

paper_verbatim: ten datasets including two language modeling datasets ...
notes: Spec eval set differs (WikiText-2 PPL + lm-eval-harness on its own subset).

paper_verbatim: Three state-of-the-art SVD-based LLM compression methods: FWSVD, ASVD, and SVD-LLM
notes: Baselines not used.
Headline result; not reproduced in spec.
Not used.
Not used.
Not used.
Not used.

paper_verbatim: FWSVD takes about 6 GPU hours, ASVD takes about 5.5 GPU hours, SVD-LLM takes about 15 minutes, and SVD-LLM V2 takes about 18 minutes
notes: Not used.

paper_verbatim: speedup of 1.29x ... 2.71x under 80% compression ratio
notes: Not used.
Ablation not used.

paper_verbatim: the changes in both data number and sampling seed in the calibration set incur no more than 1% fluctuation
notes: Robustness claim not invoked by spec.

paper_verbatim: Number of data: 256 / 512 / 1024 / 2048; Perplexity range 7.12-7.24.
notes: Not used.
Not used.
Not used.

paper_verbatim: GPTQ-3bit 2.8 GB 16.28; SVD-LLM V2 2.8 GB 119; SVD-LLM V2 + GPTQ-4bit 2.8 GB 9.97 (↓39%)
notes: Not used.

paper_verbatim: PB-LLM 1-bit 1.9 GB 104.83; BiLLM 1-bit 1.5 GB 47.67; SVD-LLM V2 16-bit 1.5 GB 99.64; SVD-LLM V2 2-bit 1.5 GB 14.73 (↓69%)
notes: Not used.

paper_verbatim: throughput speedup of up to 2.71× compared to the original LLMs on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU.
notes: Not used.

paper_verbatim: by combining with 2-bit quantization, SVD-LLM V2 is able to outperform 1-bit BiLLM
notes: Quantization stacking not in spec scope.

paper_verbatim: under 90% compression ratio, there is a small performance gap compared with state-of-the-art quantization methods.
notes: Not used.

spec_paraphrase: Spec D7a operates per (layer, matrix_type) pair: D-Rank Eq. 7 produces a single k_g per (layer, matrix_type) group; per-projection bias multiplies that group rank.
paper_verbatim: groups the weight matrices across all the layers in the original LLM by type ... assigns a unique compression ratio to each weight matrix within each group based on the computed truncation loss.
notes: V2 group = projection-type bucket spanning all layers; spec group = (layer, projection-type) pair. Spec is finer-grained than V2 but consistent with the V2 spirit. V/O/Down implicit-grouping caveat captured here per audit instruction.

paper_verbatim: LG ← 1/Log(LG) ▷ Normalize LG
notes: 1/log normalization rule not used; spec uses spectral entropy formula from D-Rank.

paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 bypasses the Cholesky decomposition ... SVD on XX^T ... another round of SVD on W × Us × √Ss
notes: AA-SVD (2604.02119) used instead, which uses eigh of B-covariance and a single weighted SVD.

paper_verbatim: The final compressed weight matrix W' can be obtained via Uws × Trunc.(Sws) × S_s^{-1} × U_s^{-1}
notes: Vws-factor discrepancy captured per audit instruction: Algorithm 2 pseudocode omits Vws but Theorem 3.1 statement includes it (W' = Uws · Trunc(Sws) · Vws · √Ss^{-1} · Us^{-1}). Likely paper typo in Algorithm 2; correct form (per the theorem and the proof in Eq. 3) includes Vws. Spec doesn't implement V2 truncation so this discrepancy has no spec impact.

paper_verbatim: Since XX^T is the symmetric matrix ... we have Us = Ux and √Ss = Sx.
notes: Proof scaffolding not used.

spec_paraphrase: Per-projection rank bias motivation in spec relies on the empirical finding that uniform-ratio compression is suboptimal across layers/types — the canonical V2 figure 3 message.
paper_verbatim: the truncation loss varies at different layers ... indicating the 27th layer should be compressed under a smaller compression ratio, while a larger compression ratio should be applied to the first layer.
notes: Canonical motivation citation. Spec D7a's actual numerical recipe (gate=1.33/up=0.67/down=1.0) is sourced from jangq's GGUF, NOT from V2 — V2 is qualitative motivation only. D7a TODO marks the values as empirical-pending.

paper_verbatim: Number of data: 256 512 1024 2048 ... Seed for Random Sampling: 3 17 42 100
notes: Ablation sweep not used.

paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 is orthogonal to other post-training LLM compression methods, including quantization and pruning.
notes: Orthogonality framing implicit in the spec's stage-stacked pipeline (pruning → merging → SVD → EoRA → KD) but not cited from V2.

paper_verbatim: It then inverses and normalizes Lmin by 1/Log(Lmin).
notes: Not used.

paper_verbatim: existing SVD-based LLM compression methods either overlook this variation or require resource-intensive operations to determine the specific compression ratios
notes: Spec uses spectral entropy (cheap) for rank allocation and a separate WikiText-2 PPL search only for Swift-SVD+ α (D9-resolved).
